key_insight: The paper turns conservation-preserving mixed finite elements into a Gaussian-process-based surrogate, enabling real-time PDE prediction with closed-form uncertainty estimates.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast, structure-aware alternative to traditional solvers for verification, validation, and decision-making in PDE-driven engineering workflows.
method: Builds an $H(\mathrm{div})$--$L^2$ reduced subspace using Raviart--Thomas and $dgP_0$ elements selected by a lightweight transformer.
Trains flux-to-state dynamics via an optimal recovery formulation, converting GP regression into a constrained optimization problem with conservation equality constraints.
Uses Schur-complement training and a Dirichlet-to-Neumann map to obtain closed-form posterior flux uncertainty for quantities of interest.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing that the posterior distribution accurately serves as a surrogate for error estimation and uncertainty quantification.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful compatibility between learned subspaces, conservation constraints, and mixed FEM discretization, which may complicate implementation and scaling.
paper_title: Structure-Preserving Neural Surrogates with Tractable Uncertainty Quantification
